integrity_page_intro: Concentration of contracted funds per operational programme, beneficiaries who appear across multiple programmes, and beneficiaries currently on the АОП debarred-suppliers register. Read together with /funds/political — these are statistical risk flags, not in themselves accusations of wrongdoing.
integrity_programmes_hint: Operational programmes with at least one contract in ИСУН
integrity_high_hint: HHI ≥ 2500 — one or two beneficiaries dominate
integrity_debarred_hint: ИСУН beneficiaries currently on the АОП debarred-suppliers register
integrity_concentration_intro: HHI sums the squared market shares of every beneficiary in the programme. Above 2500 is the standard antitrust threshold for high concentration.
integrity_serial_title: Serial winners — top beneficiaries across multiple programmes
integrity_serial_intro: Beneficiaries who place in the top-10 of two or more operational programmes — single companies tapping multiple parallel funding streams.
integrity_debarred_intro: Suppliers currently on the АОП debarred-suppliers register who also appear as EU-funds beneficiaries in ИСУН. Name-matched — verify identity before publication.
integrity_no_data: Integrity data is unavailable. Run the funds:ingest-projects pipeline.
integrity_disclaimer: HHI / serial-winner / debarred flags are statistical signals only — read together with /funds/political and the per-programme drill-down before treating any single flag as a finding.
integrity_teaser_intro: Programmes where one or two beneficiaries dominate, beneficiaries who win across multiple programmes, and АОП debarred suppliers found in ИСУН.
focus_tile_intro: Editorial lenses across the EU-funds corpus — guest houses, roads, agriculture, schools, municipal infrastructure. Each opens a focused dashboard with top beneficiaries, programmes, geography and investigative-journalism sources.
focus_not_found: This focus theme is not defined.
rrf_page_description: Bulgaria's National Recovery and Resilience Plan — allocation, contracted, paid, and the 2026 disbursement deadline.
rrf_page_intro: The Recovery and Resilience Plan (ПВУ) is Bulgaria's slice of NextGenerationEU, with a hard August 2026 disbursement deadline. The figures below combine the ИСУН public register (contracted + paid) with the EC scoreboard envelope.
rrf_allocation_hint: Total RRF allocation after the 2024 amendment (grants + loans)
rrf_contracted_hint: Sum of all ПВУ contract values registered in the public register
rrf_paid_hint: Actually disbursed to beneficiaries per ИСУН
rrf_paid_vs_allocation_hint: What share of the EC envelope has actually been paid out — the EU benchmark metric
rrf_ec_intro: For cross-country comparison (BG vs the EU average) and the official 100-largest-recipients list per Member State, use the EC's Recovery and Resilience Scoreboard.
rrf_view_programme: Open the full ПВУ programme drill-down →
rrf_no_data: RRF data is unavailable. Run the funds:ingest-projects pipeline.
rrf_teaser_intro: Bulgaria's NextGenerationEU envelope. The implementation deadline is August 2026.
absorption_intro: Cumulative funds paid out as a share of contracted, per EU programming period plus the Recovery & Resilience Plan. The n+3 rule forces each cycle to disburse by year-end three years after the period closes.
absorption_period_2007_13_hint: First period with full absorption should be closed by now.
absorption_period_2014_20_hint: Disbursement deadline: December 2023 (n+3). Below 95% means money lost to the budget.
absorption_period_rrp_hint: RRP — deadline 2026. BG sits below 30%, trailing the EU average.
funds_sankey_intro: Each structural fund (ERDF, ESF, Cohesion, EAFRD, JTF + the Recovery Plan) and its top operational programmes by contracted EUR. Ribbon width is proportional to contracted EUR. Click a programme name to drill into its top beneficiaries.
funds_political_intro: EU-funds beneficiaries whose declared owners or managers are sitting MPs, cabinet members, regional governors, mayors, or councillors. Source: Сметна палата declarations + Commerce Registry.
funds_political_mp_only_hint: Sitting or former MP business connection
funds_political_official_only_hint: Cabinet, governor, mayor, councillor or agency head
funds_political_both_hint: Both an MP and a non-MP official
funds_political_disclaimer: A connection describes what the official is on record for — not in itself an accusation of wrongdoing.
funds_political_page_description: EU-funds beneficiaries whose declared owners or managers are politically-exposed persons.
funds_political_page_intro: Every EU-funds beneficiary whose declared owners or managers include a sitting MP, cabinet member, deputy minister, state-agency head, regional governor, mayor, deputy mayor, council chair, councillor, or chief architect. Source: Сметна палата declarations and the Commerce Registry.
funds_political_total_hint: Companies with a declared MP or officials linkage in ИСУН
funds_political_debarred_hint: Currently on the АОП debarred-suppliers register
funds_political_none: No politically-tied beneficiaries are currently flagged.
company_political_intro: This beneficiary's declared owners or managers are politically-exposed persons (MPs, cabinet, governors, mayors, councillors). Information from Сметна палата declarations + Commerce Registry — not in itself an accusation of wrongdoing.
funds_tile_more_below: Showing top beneficiaries by funds contracted; click “See all” for the full list.
funds_tile_source_hint: Source: ИСУН 2020. Joined to MP business filings (cacbg + TR).
funds_no_connected_long: No EU-funds beneficiaries were found for companies connected to this candidate. Either the MP's known business graph doesn't intersect the ИСУН register, or no such company has signed an EU-funds contract.
funds_page_intro: Each row is a company that received an EU-funds contract AND has a recorded business linkage (Commerce Registry role or property-declaration stake) to this MP.
funds_page_source_hint: Source: ИСУН 2020 public beneficiary register. MP linkages from cacbg property declarations and Commerce Registry filings. Connections describe what the MP has declared or is on record for — they are not in themselves an accusation of wrongdoing. “Contracted funds” is the total contracted project value per ИСУН — for some programmes it includes the beneficiary’s own co-financing, not only the EU grant.
budget_kfp_source: Министерство на финансите via data.egov.bg — Консолидирана фискална програма (КФП), monthly state-budget execution snapshots
budget_law_dv_source: Държавен вестник — annual Закон за държавния бюджет (HTML), per-spending-unit appropriations at admin + program grain
budget_interim_law_source: Държавен вестник — interim "Закон за събирането на приходи и извършването на разходи" (удължителен закон), the bridging law adopted when no annual State Budget Law is in force at the start of a fiscal year. FY2026 ran on one (обн. ДВ бр. 113/23.12.2025, amended ДВ бр. 30/27.03.2026) after the December-2025 draft budget was withdrawn following nationwide protests. Catalogued in the budget-journey index for provenance in place of the missing State Budget Law; carries no per-spending-unit appropriation tables.
budget_ministry_execution_source: Per-ministry annual "Отчет за изпълнението на програмния бюджет" (PDF / XLSX-in-ZIP / DOCX / DOCX-in-ZIP), published by each first-level spending unit. Cloudflare-blocked sites (МФ, МВР) use a manual-pdf cache path — МФ FY2023 was backfilled from the Internet Archive. JS-rendered ministry sites (МВнР, МК, МОН, МРРБ, МЕ, МС, МТС) are surveyed via a Playwright discovery tool plus Wayback-CDX watchers; МВнР FY2023 is ingested from its ZIP-packaged XLSX. Personnel-data coverage: ~30% of total first-level FY2024 expenditure plus МФ and МВнР for FY2023; МО redacts staffing as classified; the rest of the gap is ministries that don't publish their programme-budget execution report at a stable URL
budget_ec_eu_funds_source: European Commission — "EU spending and revenue" per-Member-State XLSX (Bulgaria totals of EU expenditure and national contribution)
budget_cofog_source: Eurostat gov_10a_exp — general-government expenditure by COFOG-99 function (sector S13, annual). Covers state budget plus social-security funds (NOI / NHIF), municipal budgets and EU funds — broader than the state-budget execution feed.
budget_eu_peers_source: Eurostat gov_10a_main — general-government revenue, expenditure and balance as % of GDP (sector S13, annual). Powers the EU peer-comparison chart on /governance and the BG-vs-EU27 rank chips on the /budget headline cards (Bulgaria's position within the 27 member states + EU27 average).
budget_customs_revenue_source: Агенция "Митници" — "Митническа хроника" annual report (PDF). Itemises excise revenue by product group (fuels → diesel/petrol/LPG/natural-gas/kerosene + tobacco + alcohol), import VAT total + by BEC commercial category, and customs duties total + top-5 country of origin. Hand-curated URLs per fiscal year; output under data/budget/revenue_breakdown/customs/. Reconciles to KFP excise + customs duties totals exactly across 2022-2025. The per-product excise split also anchors the budget simulator's diesel/petrol/cigarettes/spirits excise levers.
budget_nap_annual_source: НАП — "Годишен отчет за дейността на Националната агенция за приходите" (PDF, approved by Council of Ministers in March of T+1). Carries declared net VAT by 21 КИД-2008 economic sectors (Table 3), PIT receipts by income type — employment / freelance / final tax + dividends — and Table 9 employment-PIT due contributions by sector (Jan-Nov coverage). Drives data/budget/revenue_breakdown/vat/ and data/budget/revenue_breakdown/pit/.
budget_consumption_coicop_source: Eurostat — household final consumption expenditure by COICOP purpose (nama_10_co3_p3, scaled forward by the nama_10_gdp household totals). The consumption base of the tax-policy simulator's VAT model: each category is mapped to its statutory VAT regime and the model is calibrated against actual ДДС revenue. Drives data/budget/revenue_breakdown/consumption.json.
budget_taxag_source: Eurostat — main national-accounts tax aggregates (gov_10a_taxag). The compulsory social-contribution aggregates (employees D613CE, self-employed D613CS) behind the tax-policy simulator's МОД-cap estimate — the capped insurable base recovered from contributions actually received by general government. Feeds data/budget/derived/policy_baseline.json.
budget_ses_source: Eurostat — Structure of Earnings Survey, hourly earnings deciles (earn_ses_hourly, 4-yearly waves). The D1/median/D9 ratios anchor the shape of the fitted earnings distribution behind the simulator's progressive-tax and МОД scenarios — only ratios are used, so the currency denomination cancels.
budget_noi_sod_source: НОИ — "Среден осигурителен доход" annual analysis. The average insurable income of трета-категория employees anchors the level of the fitted earnings distribution; the model is validated by reproducing НАП's employment income-tax line at the flat 10%.
budget_teachers_source: Eurostat — classroom teachers by ISCED level (educ_uoe_perp01). The school-teacher headcount (ISCED 1–3) behind the tax-policy simulator's teachers' 125%-of-average-wage spending lever.
budget_noi_statb_source: НОИ — статистически бюлетин (STATB, quarterly XLS). Distribution of pensioners by basic-pension size band plus counts exactly at each legal minimum — the data behind the simulator's минимална-пенсия (pension-floor) lever. The filename changes per quarter; the curated NOI_STATB_URL is bumped when a new bulletin publishes.
budget_noi_yearbook_source: НОИ — pension statistical yearbook (annual ZIP of chapter workbooks). The data behind the /pensions view: average pension and cash-vs-bank payment by oblast (28 ТП), the pensioner size distribution (minimum-pension spike, cap wall), and the national wage / insurable-income / pension series. Parsed by scripts/budget/noi/parse_yearbook_xlsx.ts → data/budget/noi/pensions.json.
budget_kfn_source: КФН (Financial Supervision Commission) — quarterly private-pension statistics (pillars 2 & 3). Per-fund net assets and insured persons for the УПФ / ППФ / ДПФ funds, the funded 'missing half' beside НОИ's pay-as-you-go pillar 1. Parsed by scripts/budget/kfn/parse_kfn.ts → data/budget/kfn/funds.json.
budget_nsi_wages_source: НСИ — average annual wage by economic activity (NACE-A21) × ownership (open-data id=612, JSON-stat). The public-education vs all-economy wage pair behind the teachers' 125%-of-average-wage lever.
budget_iisda_doklad_source: ИИСДА — "Доклад за състоянието на администрацията", an annual report on the state of the Bulgarian administration published by the Council of Ministers (PDF). Carries total positions (щатна численост) and filled/vacant counts across central + territorial administration, headcount by administration type (Министерства, Държавни агенции, Изпълнителни агенции, Областни администрации, Общински администрации, …), and NSI list-headcount (excludes МВР + МО). Covers 2017-2025. Combined with per-programme "Численост на щатния персонал" rows extracted from each ministry's program-budget execution report, it powers the Personnel tile on /budget, the Sankey drill-down, and the per-programme headcount + average annual cost per FTE on each ministry detail page.
budget_municipal_transfers_source: Държавен вестник — State Budget Law, Чл. 53 (Чл. 51 in older laws). The per-municipality state-to-municipal transfer table allocates the annual envelope across 265 общини × 5 categories (delegated state activities, equalization, winter road maintenance, capital subsidy, other targeted local activities). Parsed from the law HTML with phrase-anchored matching that survives article-number drift across years. Drives the "Общини" drill-down on /budget, the region dashboard tile, the per-municipality tile, and the Sofia city tile. 2025 totals reconcile to the lead-paragraph envelope at €0 delta across all five types.
budget_investment_program_source: Държавен вестник — Инвестиционна програма за общински проекти (Приложение № 3). Annual PDF annex to the State Budget Law (Чл. 113 in 2025 / Чл. 107 in 2024) listing every project the central budget funds at the municipal level. The 2025 annex has 3066 projects totalling ~€3.6B with stable OP-YY.NNN-NNNN IDs. Drives the "Капиталови разходи" drill-down on /budget with a 9-category classification (roads / water-sewage / education / social / sports / culture / buildings / energy / other) and a 14-oblast list. Parser handles justified per-glyph rendering, hyphenated line-break artifacts, and Sofia capital variants.
budget_municipal_execution_source: data.egov.bg — per-municipality касово изпълнение по ЕБК. The MINFIN B3 cash-execution report (Уточнен план vs Отчет, revenue & expense by economic paragraph) for the общини that publish it to the open-data portal — currently Русе and Николаево. Revenue is own-source (local taxes, fees, property income); the gap to expense is covered by state transfers + carry-over. Drives the municipal cash-execution tile on /budget.
budget_noi_b1_source: НОИ — Национален осигурителен институт. Monthly B1 per-fund cash-execution reports (BIFF8 XLS). Three funds: 5500 ДОО (main social-security fund), 5591 Учителски пенсионен фонд, 5592 Гарантирани вземания на работниците и служителите. The aggregated sheet carries the expenditure roll-up (Personnel / Operations / Social expenses / Subsidies / Capital); the detailed sheet has the §4100 "Пенсии" vs §4200 "Текущи трансфери, обезщетения и помощи" split. Drives the "Социалноосигурителни фондове" drill-down on /budget. 2024 verified: revenue €6.66B, expenditure €12.64B, pensions €11.13B (88% of DOO).
budget_capital_sofia_source: Sofia city — annual капиталова програма (Приложение №3 to the city budget). XLSX with a per-project capital list; §51-§55 paragraph tree, function/activity hierarchy, and 24 райони tagged in free-text descriptions. Drives the per-район Capital Projects tile on every settlement page within Столична община. 2025: 352 projects, €327.6M, 63% район-tagged.
budget_capital_plovdiv_source: Plovdiv — annual капиталова програма (Разчет за финансиране на капиталовите разходи). Borderless PDF with a vertical-text "Разпоредител с бюджет" column and explicit tagging of the 6 райони (Централен, Източен, Западен, Северен, Южен, Тракия). Drives the all-райони breakdown tile on the Plovdiv city page. 2025: 626 projects, €71.4M.
budget_capital_burgas_source: Burgas — annual капиталова програма (one sheet inside the city's draft-budget XLSX workbook). Seven funding-source columns (central-budget subsidy, own funds, debt, EU funds, other sources, two carry-over flavours). Not районирана; sub-locations come from a list of 11 villages + city quarters aligned with Wikipedia category Квартали на Бургас. Drives the funding-mix + per-settlement tile. 2025: 104 projects, €86.7M, 40% EU-financed.
budget_capital_stara_zagora_source: Stara Zagora — annual капиталова програма (Приложение №4 to the council's budget decision). Born-digital PDF inside the budget-appendices ZIP. Not районирана; 51 villages of obshtina СЗР31 are tagged via the "с." prefix in project names. Drives the per-village + top-projects tile. 2025: ~317 projects, €29.3M.
budget_capital_ruse_source: Ruse — annual капиталов разчет (year-end revised plan + execution XLSX, published end of February of T+1). Not районирана; each of the 12 villages + the satellite town Мартен has its own sheet in the workbook, so per-village attribution is via file structure (no free-text matching needed). Drives the per-village + top-projects tile. 2025: ~1050 projects, €49.5M revised plan.
budget_capital_varna_source: Varna — Appendix №4 of the city council's draft budget (scanned PDF, 71 pages). Districted municipality with 5 райони (Одесос, Приморски, Младост, Аспарухово, Владислав Варненчик). The source is rasterized, so the parser has a Gemini Vision OCR pre-step that runs once per fiscal year and caches the extracted rows under raw_data/. Drives the 5-райони breakdown tile + top projects.
budget_capital_pleven_source: Pleven — Annexes №4 and №10А to the draft budget (single 63-page docket PDF; capital pages on 13-17 and 35-37). Not districted. Source text is technically extractable but the layout is heavily fragmented — the parser slices only the capital pages with pypdf, then runs Gemini Vision OCR for clean row recognition. Drives the per-funding-source mini-grid (Carry-overs / Targeted subsidy / Other budget / EU projects) + per-settlement tile. Sum of itemised projects matches the published totals exactly (7.59M + 11.00M BGN).
budget_capital_sliven_source: Sliven — capital expenditure financing schedule (initial plan, scanned PDF on mun.sliven.bg, 23 pages). Tier-2 oblast capital with 45 settlements (city + 1 small town Кермен + 43 villages). The source is rasterized so the parser has a Gemini Vision OCR pre-step. Drives the per-settlement breakdown tile + top projects. First ingest with 60% village-level localisation — highest in the fleet.
budget_capital_dobrich_source: Dobrich-grad — capital expenditure programme (latest actualisation). Tier-2 oblast capital; single-settlement município (only the city — its surrounding 68 villages are a separate "Добрич-селска" rural município). Source is an inline HTML table on dobrich.bg, scraped server-side — no OCR / PDF parsing needed. Drives the per-funding-source mini-grid (Own funds / Targeted subsidy / EU / Carry-overs) + top projects.
budget_capital_asenovgrad_source: Asenovgrad — capital expenditure financing schedule (born-digital PDF, 10 pages on asenovgrad.bg). Plovdiv-oblast município with 29 settlements (city + 28 villages). The parser goes through Gemini Vision OCR for robustness. Drives the per-village breakdown tile + top projects (60% village-level localisation — same level as Sliven).
budget_capital_shumen_source: Shumen — Annex №6 to the budget: capital expenditure financing plan (born-digital PDF, 15 pages on shumen.bg). Oblast capital with 27 settlements (city + 26 villages). The capital PDF URL was discovered via the Playwright budget-portal harvester (Shumen's budget portal is JS-rendered). Parser goes through Gemini Vision OCR. Drives the per-village breakdown tile + top projects.
budget_capital_vidin_source: Vidin — 2022 and 2023 capital expenditure execution reports (.doc packaged inside a year-end RAR archive on vidin.bg). Oblast capital with 34 settlements (city + town of Dunavtsi + 32 villages). The operator fetches the RAR, extracts it with `unar`, converts the .doc via macOS `textutil`, then runs vidin.ts. This is an execution (not plan) report — hence the tile is titled "Capital expenditures". 90%+ village-level localisation — highest in the fleet, because every Vidin bullet ends with an explicit "гр./с. <name>" before the amount. NB: 2024 is not yet published on vidin.bg; 2025 is only published as a plan (Council Resolution №94 — a scanned PDF) whose annex itemises only the ЦС / Targeted Subsidy portion (≈2.89M of 7.47M total), so the tile stays anchored to 2023.
budget_capital_veliko_tarnovo_source: Veliko Tarnovo — Annex 15 to the municipal budget: Investment programme (clean XLSX, sheet "Pril15" inside the master "Приложения 1-22" file on veliko-tarnovo.bg). Tier-2 oblast capital with 89 settlements (city + town Debelets + town Kilifarevo + 86 villages). No OCR — data extracted directly from the spreadsheet structure. Covers 2024 (162 projects, ~€28.3M) and 2025 (382 projects, ~€47.1M) — both plans, not execution reports. Village-level localisation 47-70% depending on year; the city of Veliko Tarnovo holds the dominant share (~60% by amount). NB: 2023 and earlier annual budgets are no longer on the current CMS — the site was rebuilt in 2024 and older pages now return 404.
budget_capital_pernik_source: Pernik — Named list of capital expenditure objects (clean XLS on pernik.bg). Oblast capital with 24 settlements (city + town Batanovtsi + 22 villages). Covers 2024-2026 (2024 + 2025 in BGN; 2026 post-euro in EUR at the 1.95583 peg). No OCR. 2026 — 159 projects, ~€40.7M; 2025 — 112 projects, ~€14.0M; 2024 — 114 projects, ~€21.0M. Village-level localisation 62-74% depending on year; the city of Pernik holds ~70% by amount.
budget_capital_haskovo_source: Haskovo — Annex №7 to the municipal budget: Capital expenditure financing plan (19-page born-digital landscape PDF on haskovo.bg). Oblast capital with 37 settlements (city + 36 villages). The parser goes through Gemini Vision OCR for robust joining of long multi-line project names spread across narrow funding-source columns. Drives the per-village breakdown tile + top projects.
budget_capital_gabrovo_source: Gabrovo — Annex №5 to the municipal budget: Investment programme (9-page born-digital landscape PDF on gabrovo.bg). Oblast capital with 134 settlements (city + 133 villages — the largest village count in the fleet). The PDF URL was discovered via Google indexing — gabrovo.bg's portal is JS-rendered and direct curl/grep doesn't surface the file. OCR via Gemini Vision. 2025: 299 projects, ~€8.1M (December actualisation).
budget_capital_yambol_source: Yambol — Annex 4 (2022-2024) / Annex 5 (2025) to the municipal budget: Capital expenditure financing plan. The file is a born-digital PDF packaged inside a RAR (2024+) or ZIP (2022-23) archive on yambol.bg/byudzhet. Yambol is a single-settlement município — just the city. OCR via Gemini Vision. Covers 4 fiscal years (2022-2025): 110-184 projects, ~€7.0-13.8M per year. All 4 years' itemised sums match the published recap exactly (ratio 1.000).
budget_capital_kardzhali_source: Kardzhali — Capital expenditure financing plan. PDF URL discovered via Google indexing (kardjali.bg's portal is undocumented). 118 settlements (city + 117 villages). The file is a born-digital Excel-rendered PDF in two variants: PROJECT (early-year proposal) and ACTUALISATION (with било/става amendment-pair columns — parser captures СТАВА). OCR via Gemini Vision. 2024 (3rd actualisation, Dec): 160 projects, ~€18.7M; 2025 (project, April): 104 projects, ~€16.7M. Both years match the published recap exactly (ratio 1.000).
budget_capital_lovech_source: Lovech — "Budget and capital expenditures of Lovech municipality for 2025" (lovech.bg). 77-page scanned Konica Minolta PDF covering the full budget resolution. The capital list sits on pages 36-42 (landscape, multi-column funding-source breakdown). Operator slices those pages with pypdf, then runs Gemini Vision OCR. The multi-column layout causes occasional OCR mis-pick of a multi-year column, so the parser overrides the headline with the council's authoritative total from Resolution №465 (49,781,917 BGN); the per-village breakdown is preserved for relative rankings (amounts approximate). 35 settlements (city + 34 villages).
budget_capital_dupnitsa_source: Dupnitsa — Capital expenditure financing plan (born-digital MINFIN B3 PDF on dupnitsa.bg/section-316). Not an oblast capital — a Kyustendil-oblast município with 17 settlements (city + 16 villages). URL discovered via Google indexing; the file routes through a PHP service-endpoint that needs a Referer header. OCR via Gemini Vision. 2025 (Sept snapshot): 137 projects, ~€6.7M, perfect recap match (ratio 1.000). 42% village-level localisation.
budget_capital_velingrad_source: Velingrad — Draft Capital Expenditure Programme (born-digital PDF on m.velingrad.bg mobile subdomain). Not an oblast capital — a Pazardjik-oblast município with 21 settlements (city + 20 villages). URL discovered via Google. OCR via Gemini Vision. 2025: 159 projects, ~€19.0M, perfect recap match (ratio 1.000). 74% village-level localisation.
budget_capital_samokov_source: Samokov — Annex №5 to the municipal budget: Named list of construction, major-repair, and intangible-asset acquisition objects (born-digital Excel-rendered PDF on samokov.bg). Not an oblast capital — a Sofia-oblast município with 28 settlements (city + 27 villages, including ski resort к.к. Borovets). URL discovered via Google. OCR via Gemini Vision. 2025: 231 projects, ~€29.7M, perfect recap match (ratio 1.000). 74% village-level localisation.
budget_capital_karlovo_source: Karlovo — Annex № 7 to the municipal budget: plan for financing capital expenditure (clean XLSX from karlovo.bg's service-download endpoint, no OCR). Not an oblast capital — a Plovdiv-oblast município with 27 settlements (4 towns: Karlovo, Kalofer, Klisura, Banya + 23 villages). Workbook has two sheets; the standalone "2025" sheet is parsed (col "Обща сума за обекта" is the per-line total). 2025: 136 projects, ~€15.0M, perfect recap match with the published ОБЩО total. 81% settlement localisation (untagged rows are generic equipment lines).
budget_capital_kazanlak_source: Kazanlak — Annex №4 to the municipal budget: investment programme and current repairs ("Проект на инвестиционна програма и текущи ремонти"). Source is the council's "Приложения" PDF (born-digital, 17 pages — programme is on pages 9-17). Not an oblast capital — a Stara Zagora-oblast município with 20 settlements (3 towns: Kazanlak, Krun, Shipka + 17 villages). The site is Nuxt-rendered, so the file URL was discovered via the page's _payload.json (the accompanying Budget_2025_7404.xls file is password-protected and unusable). OCR via Gemini Vision. 2025: 201 projects, ~€7.9M, perfect recap match with "Общо за Общината". 35% settlement localisation — Kazanlak's investment programme is heavy on centrally-purchased equipment (computers, climatics, ОП КДиПИС vehicles) that doesn't carry settlement tags.
budget_capital_kyustendil_source: Kyustendil — Annex №6 to the council's "Окончателен годишен план" 2025 docket (a 41-page mixed scan + born-digital PDF on obs.kyustendil.bg/Documents/DnevenRed/30/). Oblast capital (KNL29) with 72 settlements (city + 71 villages — second-largest village count in the fleet after Gabrovo's 134). Operator pre-slices pages 30-40 into a focused PDF; OCR via Gemini Vision. 2025 final plan: 246 projects, ~€11.0M, perfect recap match with the published "ОБЩО Капиталови разходи" total. 43% settlement localisation.
budget_capital_montana_source: Montana — "Капиталова програма за 2025 г." (5-page rasterized Konica Minolta scan from montana.bg's budget portal at /свали/бюджет/32). Oblast capital (MON29) with 24 settlements (1 town + 23 villages). The PDF has 4 per-function sub-appendices on pages 1-4 (Прил. 7а/7б/7в/7д, funding-source breakdowns) and a CONSOLIDATED 9-project summary on page 5 totalling 53.9M BGN (ВСИЧКО) plus a separate 3M театър ремонт row below it. Parser reads page 5 only (pages 1-4 would double-count). Headline ≈€29.1M including the theater. OCR via Gemini Vision. 78% settlement localisation.
budget_ipop_source: MRRB — Инвестиционна програма за общински проекти (IPOP). The Ministry of Regional Development publishes a daily-refreshed CSV at ipop.mrrb.bg/reports_projects_export.php with execution status for every МРРБ-funded municipal project — 3,492 projects covering 264 of Bulgaria's 265 municipalities. Same OP-YY.NNN-NNNN project IDs as the State Budget Law's Annex III investment programme, but here every row carries agreement value, paid-to-date, submitted-in-review and approved-awaiting-payment amounts in EUR. Drives the IPOP execution tile on every município settlement page (shows total agreement, paid percentage with progress bar, pending pipeline, stalled-project count). As of late May 2026: €2.98B total agreement, €991M paid (33.2% execution), 769 projects flagged stalled (≥ €100k agreement, < 5% paid).
fiscal_reserve_minfin_xlsx_source: Министерство на финансите — Фискален резерв (monthly FRA-MM-YYYY-BG.xlsx). Authoritative end-of-month stock; unit switched from BGN to EUR with the 2026-01-01 euro adoption. Fetched via Wayback Machine where cached, manual drop otherwise.
fiscal_reserve_minfin_mreport_source: Министерство на финансите — Месечен бюлетин (mreport PDF). КФП summary table carrying the фискален резерв row across ~12 rolling months. Secondary cross-validation source.
fiscal_reserve_minfin_buletin_source: Министерство на финансите — Информационен бюлетин (BULETIN PDF). Narrative press-bulletin with the single end-of-month figure stated inline. Third cross-validation source.
dashboard_benford_hint: Number of parties whose first- or second-digit distribution of section vote counts deviates from Benford's expectation (MAD). This is a screening signal, not evidence of fraud.
dashboard_risk_score_hint: Number of sections in the Critical band on a composite score from seven statistical signals. A high score means the section warrants a closer look — not a verdict.
dashboard_risk_score_critical_suffix: sections in Critical band (of {{total}} total sections, {{pct}})
risk_analysis_description: A consolidated view of every statistical screening signal we publish for this election: section-level risk score, Benford digit distributions, voting-machine flash-memory drift, vote concentration, and at-risk neighborhoods. Each section is a summary that links to the full data.
risk_analysis_caveat_title: This is a screening tool, not a verdict
risk_analysis_caveat_body: This page combines several screening signals. Each has innocent explanations (small section size, demographic homogeneity, late registration drives, lawful recount). Always inspect the breakdown for the specific section or party before drawing conclusions.
risk_clusters_hint: Knots of physically adjacent polling sections that all screen elevated-or-above and were won by the same party — the geographic fingerprint of a controlled or corporate vote. A view over the risk scores, not a verdict.
risk_clusters_headline: {{count}} clusters of adjacent same-party sections group {{sectioned}} polling sections. {{total}} sections in total screen elevated or above — use the toggles to filter the map.
risk_clusters_headline_none: No section clusters detected. {{total}} sections screen elevated or above — use the band toggles to filter the map.
risk_history_hint: Every election this polling section took part in, side by side — winner, turnout and the risk SCREENING score. A view over published data, not a verdict.
risk_history_headline: Screened elevated or above in {{count}} of {{total}} elections.
risk_history_headline_none: No elevated screening risk in any of {{total}} elections.
risk_persistence_hint: Geographic knots of adjacent same-party sections that clustered — screened elevated or above as a bloc — in two or more elections. A knot that recurs cycle after cycle is the fingerprint of a standing vote-control operation, not a one-off. A view over the risk clusters, not a verdict.
risk_persistence_headline: {{count}} geographic knots clustered in two or more elections; {{deep}} recurred in three or more. Each dot is one election, coloured by the winning party.
risk_persistence_not_found: This cluster could not be found.
risk_persistence_detail_summary: Clustered in {{count}} elections — {{sections}} polling sections in total.
risk_persistence_sections_hint: Every polling section that belonged to the cluster in at least one election. Open one for its full cross-election risk history.
risk_persistence_problem_aggregate: {{count}} also coincide with a Roma neighbourhood already flagged for controlled-vote risk.
risk_persistence_problem_overlap: {{count}} of {{total}} sections lie in {{name}} — a flagged problem neighbourhood.
risk_analysis_sections_hint: Composite 0–100 score from seven statistical signals: flash-memory drift, recount corrections, invalid ballots, additional voters, vote concentration, peer-section outliers, shift vs. the previous election.
risk_analysis_sections_headline: {{critical}} sections fall in the Critical band ({{criticalPct}}); {{highOrAbove}} are High or Critical ({{highOrAbovePct}}).
risk_analysis_benford_hint: Parties whose section-vote digit distribution deviates from the Benford expectation. Election-forensics literature (Mebane) recommends the second-digit test (2BL) over the first-digit test for vote counts.
risk_analysis_benford_headline: {{strong}} parties with strong deviation (MAD ≥ 0.08), {{moderate}} more with moderate (0.04–0.08). {{total}} parties tested.
risk_analysis_benford_none: None of the {{total}} tested parties show significant deviation from Benford.
risk_analysis_link_risk_score_desc: 0–100 composite score per polling section, with a breakdown across the seven signals.
risk_analysis_link_benford_desc: First- and second-digit distribution of section vote counts, per party.
risk_analysis_link_flash_desc: Per-party comparison of votes recorded on the machine flash vs. on the protocols.
risk_analysis_link_concentrated_desc: Settlements where a single party receives over 80% of the valid vote.
risk_analysis_link_problem_sections_desc: Polling sections in Roma neighborhoods — votes, turnout, leading party.
risk_analysis_link_additional_voters_des: Sections with unusually many voters added to the electoral list on election day.
composite_index_hint: 0–100 composite score from five process-integrity signals (section screening, machine integrity, missing flash, concentration, procedural). Equally weighted, averaged only across components that have data. Context signals (Benford, neighborhood swing, electoral volatility, polling error) are shown alongside but do NOT feed the headline — they can light up in perfectly clean elections.
composite_index_caption: Average across {{available}} of {{total}} process-integrity components. Click any component for detail.
composite_context_hint: These signals are informational — they measure statistical fingerprints, demographic volatility, or forecast failure rather than direct irregularity. They are shown separately and do NOT feed into the headline average.
composite_component_sections_hint: Weighted share of national turnout sitting in sections with Elevated/High/Critical screening score (Critical weighted 1.0, High 0.5, Elevated 0.2). Calibrated cap — 5% of turnout = 100.
composite_component_machine_hint: Sum of absolute differences between flash-memory and protocol votes, divided by total machine votes. Calibrated cap — 0.2% drift = 100. The threshold matches international audit-trigger neighborhoods (US recount rules, Mexico INE).
composite_component_concentration_hint: Share of national turnout that went to the top party in settlements where it took ≥80% of valid votes. Calibrated cap — 2% of turnout = 100.
composite_component_procedural_hint: Combined share of national turnout from invalid ballots and additional voters in settlements where those rates exceed 10%. Calibrated cap — 2% of turnout = 100.
composite_component_benford_hint: Share of tested parties (with at least 100 sections) that show strong deviation from Benford (MAD ≥ 0.08) on the second-digit test. Mebane's recommended test for vote-count data.
composite_component_neighborhoodsSwing_h: Change in the top party's vote share inside the eight tracked communities versus the prior cycle, MINUS that party's national change over the same pair of cycles. A much cleaner signal than the static demographic share — it isolates the cycle-specific movement that the vote-buying literature (Stokes et al. 2013; Cantú 2014) associates with broker-managed turnout. Calibrated cap: 15 pp excess swing = 100.
composite_component_voteSwitching_hint: Pedersen (1979) index: 0.5 × Σ|Δshare| across parties between this cycle and the prior. The standard comparative-politics measure of net vote transfer between parties. Below 5 (stable democracies) = 0; above 30 (hyper-volatile cycles like BG 2021) = 100. High values do NOT imply irregularity — usually they signal a new entrant or major realignment.
composite_component_missingFlash_hint: Share of total machine votes sitting in sections that ran machine voting but submitted no flash drive at protocol time. These votes are not end-to-end auditable. Calibrated cap — 1% of machine votes = 100.
composite_component_polls_hint: Pollster mean absolute error (MAE) averaged across all agencies for this election. Below 1.5 pp (international baseline) = 0; above 5 pp = 100. CONTEXT signal, not integrity — high values mean forecast failure (methodology, late deciders, legal blackout window, new entrant), not election irregularity. This is why it is excluded from the headline index.
composite_component_clusters_hint: Share of the geolocatable elevated-or-above sections that fall into a same-party geographic cluster — a knot of adjacent flagged sections, the controlled/corporate-vote fingerprint. Below 5% (irreducible structural clustering) = 0; above 20% = 100. CONTEXT signal — it re-describes sections already counted by the section-screening integrity component, and clustering is partly structural, so it never feeds the headline.
risk_analysis_polls_disclaimer_short: Polling error does not imply election irregularity.
risk_analysis_polls_hint: Comparison of pollster mean absolute error for this election against the historical norm. This is a context signal — not part of the composite risk index. Polling error has structural causes (methodology, late deciders, sample representativeness) that would fire this signal even in clean elections.
risk_analysis_polls_headline_with_baseli: {{count}} agencies, mean error {{mean}} percentage points (historical mean for BG: {{baseline}}).
composite_methodology_description: How the composite index is computed — the five process-integrity signals that form the headline score, and the four context signals shown alongside.
composite_methodology_caveat_title: An analysis tool, not a verdict
composite_methodology_caveat_body: A high index value means an election is statistically unusual along several independent measures and deserves closer examination — nothing more. Each component can have a perfectly logical explanation, so always inspect the detailed breakdown before drawing conclusions.
composite_methodology_intro: The Election Risk Index is a 0–100 score that summarises five independent process-integrity signals. It sits at the top of the /risk-analysis page with the full breakdown, and in compact form on the home page under the Anomalies section. Alongside it we show four context signals (Benford, neighborhood swing, electoral volatility, polling error) that do NOT feed the headline — they can light up in perfectly clean elections.
composite_methodology_p_what1: The headline is the arithmetic mean of five process-integrity signals: section screening, machine integrity, missing flash, concentration, and procedural anomalies. Each is vote-weighted (as a share of national turnout, or of total machine votes for the machine-only signals) so it reads as "what fraction of the result this signal touches," not "how many places are flagged."
composite_methodology_p_what2: The formula applies only to available data. If older elections lack data (e.g. flash memory predates 2021), the index is computed from the remaining available process-integrity signals. Context signals are shown for every cycle where their data is available.
composite_methodology_p_what3: Splitting the signals into two tracks follows the international electoral-integrity frameworks (Pippa Norris PEI, V-Dem Election Quality Index, IFES). They do not mix process signals (audit-trail loss, protocol/flash disagreement) with context signals (statistical fingerprints, demographic volatility, forecast failure). Polling error in particular is NOT an irregularity signal — it is a prompt to inspect the process-integrity components more carefully.
composite_methodology_p_components_intro: Each component has a value from 0 to 100, and the links in the UI lead to the full source data for each. The headline is their simple average.
composite_methodology_c_sections_p: weighted share of national turnout sitting in sections with Elevated/High/Critical screening score (Critical weighted 1.0, High 0.5, Elevated 0.2). Calibrated cap — 5% of turnout = 100.
composite_methodology_c_machine_p: sum of absolute differences between flash memory and protocol per party, scaled to total machine votes. Calibrated cap — 0.2% drift = 100 (matching international audit-trigger thresholds).
composite_methodology_c_missingFlash_p: share of total machine votes sitting in sections with machine voting but missing flash data. These votes cannot be fully audited. (Cap: 1% of machine votes).
composite_methodology_c_concentration_p: share of national turnout that went to the top party in settlements where it took ≥80% of valid votes. The classic signal of a controlled or 'captured' electorate. (Cap: 2% of turnout).
composite_methodology_c_procedural_p: combined share of national turnout from invalid ballots and additional voters in settlements where those rates exceed 10%. These two factors are often signs of organised or broker-driven voting. (Cap: 2% of turnout).
composite_methodology_p_context_intro: These signals are shown in a separate row and do NOT feed the headline average. They commonly light up in perfectly clean elections (new entrants, late deciders, range-bounded vote counts that fail Benford by construction).
composite_methodology_c_benford_p: share of parties (with over 100 sections) that show strong deviation (MAD ≥ 0.08) on the second-digit test. Benford on vote counts often fails by construction (Mebane 2006), which is why it is a context signal.
composite_methodology_c_neighborhoodsSwi: change in the top party's vote share in the eight tracked communities versus the prior cycle, minus the same party's national change. Isolates cycle-specific movement that the vote-buying literature (Stokes et al. 2013; Cantú 2014) associates with broker-managed turnout. Cap: 15 pp excess swing = 100.
composite_methodology_c_voteSwitching_p: Pedersen (1979) index: 0.5 × Σ|Δshare| across parties between this cycle and the prior. Below 5 (stable democracies) = 0; above 30 (hyper-volatile cycles) = 100. High values do NOT imply irregularity — usually a new entrant or major realignment.
composite_methodology_c_polls_p: mean absolute error (MAE) of agencies for the cycle. A high value here signals forecast failure (new entrant, late deciders, legal blackout window), not an election irregularity. Excluded from the headline for that reason — but it should prompt a closer look at the process-integrity signals.
composite_methodology_c_clusters_p: share of the geolocatable elevated-risk sections that sit in a same-party geographic cluster — a knot of adjacent flagged sections won by one party. Calibrated on the 2005–2026 backtest (a stable 8–17% band): below 5% = 0, above 20% = 100. A context signal — it re-describes sections the section-screening component already counts, and clustering is partly a structural artefact of geographically correlated risk factors, so it is kept out of the headline.
composite_methodology_p_bands_intro: Bands are intentionally asymmetric, since the actual distribution in Bulgaria (2009–2026) shows that most 'clean' cycles fall in the 0–25 range.
composite_methodology_band_calm: No unusual signals on any component.
composite_methodology_band_elevated: At least one component shows mid-level deviation; typical for most post-2021 elections.
composite_methodology_band_high: Several components are simultaneously above mid-level; warrants careful analysis.
composite_methodology_band_critical: Many components are near their cap; extremely rare historically.
composite_methodology_important_caps_p: Component thresholds are calibrated to actually-observed data in 2009–2026, not to theoretical maxima.
composite_methodology_important_availabl: The headline uses all five process-integrity signals for elections from July 2021 onwards. For older cycles (before mass machine voting was introduced), 'Machine integrity' and 'Missing flash memory' are skipped and the index is computed from the remaining three. The comparison is fair but based on less information. Context signals are available only when their data exists for the cycle (neighborhood swing and electoral volatility require a prior cycle for comparison).
composite_methodology_important_polls_t: Why context signals are excluded from the headline:
composite_methodology_important_polls_p: Benford on vote counts often fails by construction (the test requires data spanning several orders of magnitude, but per-section party vote counts cluster in a narrow range). Polling error has structural causes unrelated to the electoral process (legal blackout, new entrants, late deciders). Electoral volatility measures political instability, not irregularity. Neighborhood swing is a cycle-on-cycle signal — substantive but threshold-sensitive. All four are shown, but outside the headline average.
composite_methodology_important_evidence: The index is a starting point for investigation, not a final piece of evidence. Always analyse which specific components form the final score.
data_changes_description: A log of data refreshes — what changed on which day and where to see it on the site.
data_map_description: An interactive map of every source we ingest — the datasets built from them and the site features they power. One source often feeds several features.
data_map_hint: Select a node to see where the data comes from and where it is used. The chips above switch thematic views, and a pulsing dot marks sources refreshed in the last few days.
data_map_legend_fresh: A pulsing dot marks a refresh within the last 7 days.
data_map_stories_hint: Walk the map step by step:
data_map_method: The map is generated automatically from the watched-sources registry — a new source appears here the moment it is added.
data_description: Data sources, the refresh log, and download links — all in one place.

procurement_tile_more_below: Showing top contractors by total amount; click “See all” for the full list.
procurement_tile_source_hint: Source: data.egov.bg (АОП OCDS). MP linkages from cacbg property declarations and the Commerce Registry.
procurement_page_intro: Each row is a company that received a public-procurement contract AND has a recorded business linkage to this MP (a Commerce Registry role or a declared property stake).
procurement_page_source_hint: Source: data.egov.bg (АОП OCDS). MP linkages from cacbg property declarations and Commerce Registry filings. Connections describe what the MP has declared or is on record for — they are not in themselves an accusation of wrongdoing.
procurement_no_connected_long: No public-procurement contracts were found for companies connected to this candidate. Either the MP's known business graph doesn't intersect the АОП dataset yet, or no such company won a contract during the ingested period.
procurement_index_intro: Aggregated contract awards from data.egov.bg's АОП OCDS feed. Contractors connected to a sitting or former MP are highlighted.
procurement_index_contracts_hint: Signed contracts + amendments within this election's period.
procurement_index_contractors_hint: Distinct companies that won at least one contract in the period.
procurement_index_total_hint: Total value of contracts awarded in the period.
procurement_index_connected_hint: MPs and public officials (cabinet, regional governors, mayors, councillors…) whose declared business interests intersect with contract winners during this period.
procurement_index_top_subtitle: Ranked by total contract value. Contractors linked to MPs are highlighted.
procurement_index_source_hint: Source: data.egov.bg (АОП OCDS, fortnightly bundles). MP linkages from cacbg property declarations and Commerce Registry filings.
procurement_index_unavailable: Procurement data is not available yet. Run /update-procurement to populate the dataset.
company_not_found_desc: No public-procurement or EU-funds data is recorded for this EIK. The company may not have signed a contract during the ingested period, or the EIK is incorrect.
procurement_no_contracts: No public-procurement contracts found for this entity.

procurement_settlement_intro_p1: Every signed contract published on the central procurement register (АОП) is pinned to the buyer's headquarters. We exclude central ministries, state agencies and nationally-operating state companies — their Sofia HQ tells you nothing about where the contract was spent — and aggregate them separately under "National procurement".
procurement_settlement_single_buyer_tip: The entire amount comes from a single buyer (often a state-company HQ).
procurement_settlement_table_note: “1 buyer” marks settlements whose entire total comes from a single buyer. Average contract value is shown only for settlements with at least 5 contracts.
procurement_settlement_truncated_msg: Showing the first 500 settlements — refine the search to narrow.
procurement_settlement_footnote: Buyer HQ is the location proxy. Schools, hospitals, municipalities, universities, regional government offices, forestry districts and local utilities are pinned to their HQ. National procurement (ministries, central agencies, national state companies) is rolled up separately.
procurement_settlement_not_found_body: This settlement has no local-tier procurement on record. The dataset only covers contracts whose buyer headquarters resolve to a settlement in our catalog; small villages and inactive buyers may simply be missing.
procurement_settlement_detail_footnote: Buyer HQ is the location proxy. Central ministries and national state companies based in Sofia procure nationally — they are not shown on this page; see the "national procurement" rollup on the landing page.
procurement_settlement_more_buyers: More buyers on the detail page
about_procurement_by_settlement_note: The "procurement by settlement" view (/procurement/by-settlement) pins each contract to the buyer's headquarters. The classifier excludes central ministries, state agencies and nationally-operating state companies (Sofia HQ, national footprint) — they roll up into a separate "national procurement" card. The buyer-HQ → EKATTE resolver is postal-primary against the canonical NSI settlement catalog (5,267 entries with postal codes); 99.9% of buyers in the 2026 sample resolve to a single settlement. The tier classifier uses name heuristics plus a curated EIK override table for the long tail.
about_procurement_risk_note: Every contract runs through a set of automated risk checks — we show which fired and which did not (debarred supplier, MP- or official-connected contractor, single bidder gated against the per-CPV competition baseline, non-open procedure, short tender window, post-award amendment, annex growth past the legal cap, newly-formed contractor, possible split purchasing, single-supplier concentration). The same signals drive the money-flow diagram on the dashboard (and per-entity), the connected MPs & officials rankings (/procurement/mps), the risk-flag feed (/procurement/flags) and a per-oblast choropleth (total / per-capita / average contract). All derived from the same АОП / ЦАИС ЕОП open data — no new source.
company_no_power_links: No links to people in power on record
risk_flag_mp_connected_long: Contractor is connected to an MP
risk_flag_mp_connected_hint: An MP appears as a declared officer or owner of this company.
risk_flag_amendment_hint: This row revises an earlier contract — common vehicle for value inflation outside the original procedure.
risk_flag_annex_growth_long: Value grew to the annex cap
risk_flag_annex_growth_hint: Signed value grew by ≥50% via annexes — at or over the ЗОП чл.116 ал.2 cumulative cap. A permitted inflation indexation carries its own separate cap, so this is a signal for review, not a proven breach.
risk_flag_new_firm_hint: The company was incorporated less than a year before this award — a newly-formed firm winning public money.
risk_flag_split_hint: Several direct awards to this supplier in the same CPV class and year, each under the direct-award ceiling but together over it. ЗОП чл. 20 ал. 4 permits separate recurring needs — a signal for review, not a proven breach.
risk_flag_pep_connected_long: Contractor tied to a public official
risk_flag_pep_connected_hint: A mayor, councillor, minister, governor or agency head appears as a declared officer or owner.
risk_flag_weak_competition_hint: A single bidder, or materially fewer bidders than the sector norm — awards land closer to the buyer's estimate (Fazekas/GTI).
risk_flag_direct_award_hint: Awarded without any call for competition (negotiated / single-source) — the awards that land at the estimate.
risk_flag_appeal_upheld_long: КЗК upheld an appeal against this procedure
risk_flag_appeal_upheld_hint: The Competition Protection Commission annulled the buyer's award decision — an official finding it was improper (not just a heuristic flag).
risk_flag_short_period_hint: Below the 14-day EU reference open-procedure window.
tender_risk_note: Signals about the procedure itself, calibrated on the Bulgarian corpus — an indicator for review, not proof of wrongdoing.
risk_flag_non_open_hint: Direct negotiation, negotiation without a notice, or an invitation to specific firms — no open call for bids.
risk_flag_rushed_deadline_hint: The submission window is below the norm for a competitive procedure (open procedure / public competition).
risk_flag_short_decision_hint: The contract was signed only days after the submission deadline.
risk_flag_award_over_estimate_hint: The awarded contracts sum to more than the procedure's forecast value — the estimate was overshot.
risk_flag_debarred_hint: The contractor appears on the АОП register of parties barred from public procurement by a standing decision.
risk_flag_concentration_hint: A single contractor takes an unusually large share of the buyer's total spend — a signal of dependence or preference.
risk_explain_intro: Automated risk indicators — descriptive, not a verdict. Each compares this contract against the market norm.
risk_na_generic: No source data to run this check.
risk_na_pep_connected: The connected-officials index isn't loaded for this view.
risk_na_annex_growth: No signed starting value to measure growth against.
risk_na_new_firm: The contractor's incorporation date or the award date is missing.
risk_na_appeal_upheld: No appeal data for this procedure.
risk_na_weak_competition: Number of bids submitted is unknown.

personnel_ministries_caveat: Coverage: ~30% of total first-level FY2024 expenditure (€2.7B of €8.9B). Includes only ministries whose programme-budget execution report has been ingested. MoD redacts the staffing numbers (classified); MoI and MoF FY2024 are Cloudflare-challenged (MoF FY2023 was backfilled via the Internet Archive). The remaining ~70% — including Interior, Education, Regional Development, Transport, Justice, Culture, Foreign Affairs — don't publish their programme-budget execution report at a stable URL.
personnel_drilldown_coverage: Coverage: {{covered}} of {{total}} ({{pct}}%) of the КФП Personnel line. Remainder = MOD (classified), MoI, MoF, and ministries not yet ingested.
municipalities_drilldown_coverage: State→municipal transfer envelope (Art. 53): {{covered}} of {{total}} ({{pct}}%) of the КФП Municipalities line. Remainder = municipal own revenue + non-Art. 53 transfers.
municipalities_drilldown_caveat: Article 53 of the State Budget Law sets out the annual envelope each община receives from the central budget. The five sub-categories are mutually exclusive. The КФП line additionally captures own revenue.
municipalities_by_oblast: By oblast (count = number of municipalities in oblast)
municipal_transfers_tile_intro: What this oblast's municipalities receive from the central budget. Article 53 of the State Budget Law sets the envelope split into five categories.
municipal_transfers_tile_caveat: Planned figures from Article 53 of the State Budget Law. Actual execution may differ slightly. Excludes municipal own revenue + non-Art. 53 transfers.
municipality_transfers_tile_intro: What this municipality receives from the central budget under Article 53 of the State Budget Law, split into five categories.
my_area_municipal_budget_coverage: {{n}} of 265 municipalities publish a machine-readable execution report
funds_status_tile_caveat: Filled segments show actually-paid amounts; faded extensions show the contracted envelope. Completed contracts typically close near 100% paid; in-progress and signed contracts carry the remaining absorption gap.
funds_programs_tile_caveat: Badge: disbursement rate. Green ≥80%, amber 40-80%, rose <40%. Programmes with very low disbursement either started late (2021-2027 cycle) or stalled.
funds_geo_tile_subtitle: {{pct}}% mapped to a settlement or municipality
funds_geo_tile_caveat: ИСУН's Местонахождение field is resolved against the EKATTE settlement index and municipality list. Region / national / unresolved buckets can't be attributed to a specific place — they show on /funds but not on settlement or municipality pages.
funds_map_subtitle: {{n}} municipalities · click for the per-place page
funds_map_caveat: Projects spanning several municipalities are split evenly between them. Sofia districts share a single Stolichna aggregate. Per-capita uses Census 2021 population. Municipalities with zero contracts appear in light grey.
funds_program_source_hint: Source: ИСУН 2020 Project register. All amounts in EUR. Top lists capped at 20 (beneficiaries, contracts) or 10 (municipalities) — the full corpus lives in the by-program shard.
funds_contract_source_hint: Source: ИСУН 2020 Project register. One per-contract record fetched on demand (~1 KB).

plovdiv_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from the Plovdiv municipal budget, broken down across the city's 6 districts.
plovdiv_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Plovdiv municipal-budget capital-expenditure schedule. Figures are planned; actual execution is reported at year-end. About 78% of objects are city-wide municipal-administration items with no district tag.
varna_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from Appendix №4 of the Varna municipal budget, broken down across the city's 5 districts. Source is a scanned PDF; extraction goes through Gemini Vision OCR.
varna_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Appendix №4 — Capital expenditure schedule attached to the Varna draft budget (scanned PDF). Figures are OCR-extracted; minor recognition errors are possible. Shows the sum of itemised objects.
burgas_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from the Burgas municipal budget, grouped by funding source.
burgas_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Burgas municipal-budget capital-expenditure schedule. Figures are planned; actual execution is reported at year-end. About 86% of objects are city-wide with no sub-location specified.
stara_zagora_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from the Stara Zagora municipal budget (Appendix №4 to the council's budget decision).
stara_zagora_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Appendix №4 to the Stara Zagora draft budget. Figures are planned; actual execution is reported at year-end. Shows the sum of itemised line-item objects. The published "КАПИТАЛОВИ РАЗХОДИ - ОБЩО" figure in Appendix №4 also includes city-wide commitments and paragraph-level rollups that don't decompose to individual objects.
ruse_capital_tile_intro: Per-object list of capital investments from the Ruse municipal budget (year-end revised plan + execution). Each village kmetstvo (Басарбово, Николово, …) has its own sheet in the source workbook, so per-village attribution is via workbook structure rather than free-text matching.
ruse_capital_tile_caveat: Source: annual XLSX from obshtinaruse.bg. Shows the revised plan for the year — actual execution may differ.
muni_exec_tile_intro: Plan vs. actual for the full fiscal year, from the municipality's ЕБК cash-execution report.
muni_exec_tile_intro_partial: Plan vs. actual for {{period}} (partial year), from the municipality's ЕБК cash-execution report.
muni_exec_plan_actual_note: Large figure = actual (отчет); small figure = revised plan; the % is execution against plan. Own revenue (local taxes, fees, property income) funds only part of spending — the rest is state transfers and carry-over, which is why expenditure exceeds own revenue.
pleven_capital_tile_intro: Itemised list of capital projects from Annexes №4 and №10А to the Pleven município budget. The PDF is technically text-extractable but the layout is fragmented — extraction goes through OCR with Gemini Vision for clean row recognition.
pleven_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Annexes №4 + №10А to the Pleven município budget. Sum of the itemised projects matches the published ВСИЧКО totals exactly (7.59M + 11.00M BGN).
sliven_capital_tile_intro: Itemised list of capital projects from the Sliven município capital expenditure financing schedule (initial plan). The source is a scanned PDF from mun.sliven.bg — extraction goes through Gemini Vision OCR.
sliven_capital_tile_caveat: Source: capital expenditure financing schedule of Sliven município (scanned PDF). Values are OCR-extracted; minor recognition errors possible. Shows the sum of the itemised projects.
dobrich_capital_tile_intro: Itemised list of capital projects from the Dobrich-grad capital expenditure programme (latest actualisation). Scraped directly from the HTML table on dobrich.bg.
dobrich_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Dobrich-grad capital expenditure programme (HTML table on dobrich.bg). Shows the sum of itemised projects from the adopted actualisation.

asenovgrad_capital_tile_caveat: Source: Asenovgrad município capital expenditure financing schedule. Shows the sum of itemised projects.
