montana_capital_tile_intro: 2025 capital programme — a 5-page scanned PDF on montana.bg's budget portal. Parser extracts the consolidated 9-project summary table on page 5 (pages 1-4 are funding-source sub-appendices that itemise the same projects). OCR via Gemini Vision.
montana_capital_tile_caveat: Source: "Капиталова програма за 2025 г." (montana.bg). Headline includes the 3M театър ремонт listed below the formal ВСИЧКО recap.
ipop_tile_intro: Per-project payment status for МРРБ's Инвестиционна програма за общински проекти (Ministry of Regional Development's municipal investment grants). Same OP-YY.NNN-NNNN IDs as the State Budget Law's Annex III, but with paid / submitted / awaiting amounts.
ipop_tile_caveat: Source: ipop.mrrb.bg CSV export. Daily refresh. Stalled-project flag = agreement ≥ €100k AND paid < 5% of agreement.
noi_drilldown_coverage: NOI gross expenditure {{gross}} vs. КФП net Social-security funds line {{net}}. The gap is the state subsidy + inter-system consolidation. Drilldown sums the three NOI funds (DOO, Teachers Pension, Bankruptcy Receivables).
noi_drilldown_caveat: Source: NOI per-fund B1 monthly cash-execution reports. Cut-off date shown next to year. Pension-type breakdown (old-age / disability / survivors) requires the annual yearbook — coming in a later release.
noi_pensions_by_type_caption: Source: annual NOI pension yearbook (Table 6.3). Sum reconciles to the КФП Pensions total within ~0.5% — the yearbook excludes mid-year inter-fund transfers and a handful of edge categories aggregated under "Other", which together account for the small residual.
capital_drilldown_coverage: Municipal investment program (Чл. 113 III): {{program}} across {{projectCount}} projects. Larger than the КФП Capital expenditure line ({{kfp}}) because that figure is net of intra-system transfers. Excludes ministry direct capex.
capital_drilldown_caveat: Source: Приложение № 3 към чл. 113 of the State Budget Law (DV PDF annex). Categories assigned by regex on project names — best-effort, not authoritative.
noi_tile_intro: How NOI spends its annual envelope across pensions, short-term benefits (sickness, maternity, unemployment), and operations.
noi_tile_gross_caption: NOI gross expenditure (DOO + Teachers Pension + Bankruptcy Receivables)
investment_tile_intro: Per-project capital allocations to municipalities under the State Budget Law's investment program (Приложение III). Classified by project type via regex on the project name.
cabinet_fiscal_footprint_intro: Capital investment program + social-security spending across every fiscal year that overlapped this cabinet's tenure. These envelopes were set by the prevailing State Budget Law — the cabinet that adopted the law and the one that executed it may differ.
cabinet_fiscal_footprint_caveat: Sums data from data/budget/investment_program/ (Приложение III to the State Budget Law) and data/budget/noi/ (NOI B1 monthly cash-execution reports). Coverage = fiscal years for which an ingest exists.
personnel_ministry_source_caveat: Source: Program Budget Execution Report — Personnel line and Staffing row. Average cost includes employer social-security contributions.
budget_index_description: Bulgarian state budget execution — revenue, spending and the deficit, from data.egov.bg.
budget_index_no_data: No budget data available yet. Run the update-budget skill to ingest it.
budget_index_source_hint: Source: Ministry of Finance — state budget execution by major budget indicators, published on
budget_scope_state: State budget (consolidated fiscal programme), execution as of
budget_revenue_hint: Tax + non-tax revenue, grants and donations collected so far this fiscal year.
budget_expenditure_hint: Spending and transfers executed so far this fiscal year.
budget_eu_hint: Bulgaria's contribution to the common EU budget.
budget_balance_hint: Revenue minus expenditure and the EU contribution. Negative = deficit.
budget_maastricht_over: above the Maastricht ceiling (3% of GDP)
budget_maastricht_under: within the Maastricht ceiling (3% of GDP)
budget_peers_subtitle: General-government revenue, expenditure and balance as % of GDP. Latest annual Eurostat figure, with a 6-year sparkline.
budget_peers_maastricht_hint: Negative = deficit. Maastricht ceiling is -3% of GDP.
budget_peers_source: Source: Eurostat gov_10a_main (annual general government main aggregates, sector S13, % of GDP).
budget_citizen_subtitle: Where the state's revenue comes from and where its spending goes, scaled so each side sums to €100.
budget_ministries_no_programs: Program breakdown not published for this unit.
budget_same_point_title: At this point in the year
budget_same_point_caption: Cumulative execution at the same calendar month, fiscal year over fiscal year. Plan share uses the annual budget law plan; nominal deltas are not inflation-adjusted.
budget_trend_caption: Cumulative execution within each fiscal year — figures reset each January.
budget_trend_caption_projected: Cumulative execution within each fiscal year — figures reset each January. Dashed lines extend the in-progress year through December using the prior year's seasonal pattern; the December endpoint matches the headline projection.
budget_flow_intro: Where the money comes from and where it goes. The two diagrams meet at the balance bridge — when spending exceeds revenue, the hatched wedge marks the gap closed by financing.
budget_flow_deficit_hint: Spending exceeds revenue — the gap is closed by financing (borrowing or reserves).
budget_flow_surplus_hint: Revenue exceeds spending — the surplus reduces debt or funds reserves.
budget_flow_source_hint: Built from the КФП monthly snapshot. Subtotal hierarchy is reconstructed from the source's flat label column.
budget_functional_subtitle: General government expenditure by function, fiscal year
budget_functional_caption: Source: Eurostat gov_10a_exp (general government, sector S13). Includes state budget, social-security funds (NSSI/NHIF) and municipal budgets — broader than the state-budget execution feed.
budget_tax_bill_above_cap_hint: Above the МОД cap each extra euro is taxed at only 10% — the effective rate falls as the salary rises.
budget_tax_bill_include_employer: Include employer contributions in the breakdown below
budget_tax_bill_vat_note: 20% VAT embedded in everyday spending, assuming {{pct}} of net income goes to standard-rated goods.
budget_tax_bill_pension_into: Paid into the pension system: {{total}}{{per}} (you {{you}} + employer {{employer}}).
budget_tax_bill_pension_into_self: Paid into the pension system: {{total}}{{per}} (you remit the full amount).
budget_tax_bill_pension_ages: Retirement in 2026: women at 62y 6m (36y 10m of service), men at 64y 9m (39y 10m of service).
budget_tax_bill_pension_caption: Simplified: pension ≈ insurable income × 1.35% × years of service, bounded by the 2026 minimum (€322) and maximum (€1,738) pension. The real formula weighs lifetime earnings against the national average.
budget_tax_bill_caption: Approximate estimate. Assumes a default-case taxpayer (born after 1959, no second-pillar opt-out). SSC stops accruing above the МОД; above the cap only the 10% income tax keeps scaling. Social-security contributions are earmarked (pension + health + unemployment), not freely allocable — the breakdown applies general-government spending shares to the whole bill purely for illustration.
budget_tax_bill_company_caption: Simplified: 10% corporate tax on profit, then 5% tax when the remainder is distributed as a dividend. Corporate tax is assessed annually; the owner's own social-security contributions are not modelled here.
budget_tax_bill_curve_title: Effective rate across the salary range
budget_tax_bill_curve_caption: Effective tax-and-contribution rate by gross salary. Once salary passes the МОД cap social security stops growing and the rate falls — the burden turns regressive at the top.
budget_tax_bill_tip_ssc: Employee social-security and health contributions — pension 8.78%, health 3.20%, sickness/maternity 1.40%, unemployment 0.40%. Charged on insurable income up to the МОД cap.
budget_tax_bill_tip_mod: Maximum insurable income (МОД) — the monthly ceiling above which no further social-security contributions are due. Income tax still applies to the whole salary.
budget_tax_bill_tip_rate: Effective rate = total tax and contributions ÷ gross income. Marginal rate = the tax on the next euro earned; it falls to 10% above the МОД cap, where social security stops.
budget_tax_bill_tip_wedge: Tax wedge — the share of the full cost of employing you (gross salary plus employer contributions) taken by tax and social security rather than reaching your take-home pay.
budget_tax_calculator_page_description: Estimate your income tax, social-security contributions and VAT for an employee, self-employed person or company owner — and see which government functions your tax bill funds.
budget_tax_calculator_page_intro: Pick a taxpayer profile and income to see your monthly tax and social-security bill, the VAT embedded in your spending, and how it maps onto general-government spending — plus a projection of your future pension.
budget_citizen_tax_calc_link: What did your taxes buy? Open the tax calculator
budget_citizen_policy_sim_link: What if a tax rate changes? Open the budget simulator
budget_policy_page_description: What happens to Bulgaria's budget balance if taxes (VAT, the flat income tax, corporate, dividends, the МОД cap, excise) or spending (pensions, wages, social benefits, subsidies) change? Move a slider and see the dynamic estimate (behavioral response + uncertainty band) — and the effect on a worked payslip.
budget_policy_page_intro: Move a slider and see two numbers at once: the change in the consolidated budget balance per year, and the change on one worked payslip per month. The default estimate is dynamic — with behavioral response and an uncertainty band; static mode (fixed base, no response) is one tap away.
budget_policy_methodology_link: How the simulator works: full methodology, sources and limitations
budget_policy_error: The policy baseline data could not be loaded — please try again later.
budget_policy_tip_vat_std: Applies to every consumption category not assigned to the reduced or zero rate. Current law: 20%.
budget_policy_tip_vat_red: The level of the reduced rate. Current law: 9%, applied to hotel accommodation, books and periodicals, baby goods.
budget_policy_tip_groups: Assign a consumption category to the standard, reduced or zero rate. Defaults reflect current law after the temporary pandemic-era cuts expired at end-2024.
budget_policy_tip_pit: The 10% flat tax on employment and other personal income. The final-tax portion (dividends to individuals, non-residents) keeps its own rate and is not rescaled.
budget_policy_tip_div: The 5% withholding on distributed dividends. Scored on the corporate-withholding budget line; dividends taxed inside personal income tax are not rescaled.
budget_policy_tip_excise: Each excise is the actual rate in its real unit, with a "like in…" EU-country quick-select (press the (i)). BG fuel sits at the EU floor; wine is a new excise on today's zero rate. Over €3.8B a year in total — more than corporate tax.
budget_policy_tip_excise_diesel: Specific excise on diesel (~€1.03B in 2025). Bulgaria sits at the EU floor, so there is no legal room to cut. Current law: €330/1000 L.
budget_policy_tip_excise_petrol: Specific excise on unleaded petrol (~€0.30B in 2025). Current law: €363/1000 L — just above the EU floor of €359.
budget_policy_tip_excise_cigarettes: Total cigarette excise (specific + a share of the price); scales the whole tobacco line (~€2.15B in 2025). The EU floor is €90/1000 and ≥60% of price. Current law: ~€114/1000. A large hike pushes sales to the illicit and cross-border market — so a steep rise can even lower revenue.
budget_policy_tip_excise_spirits: Excise on ethyl alcohol — rakia, spirits (the bulk of the ~€177M alcohol excise in 2025). The EU floor is €550/hl of pure alcohol. Current law: €562/hl. Some demand shifts to home production (the still).
budget_policy_tip_excise_wine: Bulgaria taxes wine at €0 — as does the EU minimum, so introducing one is a legal choice. The commercial market (~940,000 hl) is taxed; much home-made wine stays untaxed.
budget_policy_gambling: Gambling — fee on gross gaming revenue (GGR)
budget_policy_tip_gambling: The Gambling Act variable fee on gross gaming revenue (stakes minus payouts) for betting/lottery/online. Raised from 20% to 25% in 2026. The base (GGR ~€716M in 2025) is НАП/industry-reported — NOT a standalone budget line (gambling is an alternative tax folded into 'Corporate tax'/'Other taxes' + fees). A high rate pushes play to unlicensed/offshore operators — so a big hike can even shrink revenue. EU regimes vary widely (some tax GGR at 15–25%+, others turnover), so there is no direct 'like in…' country pick.
budget_policy_tip_vignettes: A % increase on e-vignettes only (cars ≤3.5t; base ≈€180M/year, АПИ). Near-inelastic — you must hold a vignette to drive. The 2026 framework proposed +30% (≈€53M). For readability we show the ANNUAL vignette price (official tariff BGN 97 ≈ €49.60; resellers add a fee on top); the uplift applies equally to every duration — daily, weekend, weekly, monthly, quarterly and annual.
budget_policy_tip_tolls: A % increase on the per-km toll only (trucks/buses; base ≈€380M/year, АПИ). At higher rates heavy transit can divert through neighbouring countries.
budget_policy_tip_soe: A % cut of the state-enterprise subsidy envelope (БДЖ, НКЖИ, Bulgarian Post; ≈€316M). Caveat: much is locked in multi-year public-service / infrastructure contracts, so a real operating cut near the ceiling isn't feasible — the draft budget books €285M (≈90%), but most of that is avoided indexation, not a real cut. The figure here is face value; our independent re-pricing assumes ≈35% realisation (15–55%), i.e. for a 90% cut only ≈€100M of ≈€284M is realistically bankable.
budget_policy_tip_mod: Максимален осигурителен доход — the monthly ceiling on insurable income. Raising it collects contributions on wages above the cap; lowering it forfeits them. Both directions are scored over the fitted earnings distribution, including the income-tax offset (a bigger insurable base means more deducted contributions).
budget_policy_tip_nm: Tax-free amount of the monthly taxable base (income after social-security contributions). The recurring proposal sets it at the minimum wage. Applied to employment income via the earnings distribution.
budget_policy_tip_social: Change to social spending excluding pensions (≈€4B: social & personal assistance, child & disability benefits, unemployment). One of the two main Budget-2026 deficit drivers (alongside public-sector wages). Base: COFOG social protection €15.1B minus pensions €11.1B.
budget_policy_social_compare: Total social protection: BG {{bg}}% of GDP · EU avg {{eu}}%.
budget_policy_tip_interest: Change to debt-interest spending (≈€0.7B, КФП Interest line). Rises as new debt accumulates — Budget 2026 lifts the debt to 30.1% of GDP.
budget_policy_tip_subsidies: Change to general subsidies (≈€1.0B: agriculture, energy, etc.). The БДЖ/НКЖИ/Post transport subsidies are the separate lever above.
budget_policy_preset_budget2026_tip: Loads only the CONSOLIDATION MEASURES from the draft Budget 2026 (МОД cap €2,300, vignettes +30%, an SOE-subsidy cut, the accelerated cigarette excise) — these IMPROVE the balance. IMPORTANT: the budget's actual deficit is −5.7% of GDP (€7.2B) — the highest in years — because spending (public-sector wages + social payments) grows far more than these measures save. This lever shows only the savings; use the Social benefits, Interest and wage levers to explore the spending side.
budget_policy_preset_nm_mrz_tip: The recurring trade-union proposal: no income tax on the first €620 of the monthly taxable base.
budget_policy_preset_progressive_tip: A second 20% rate on the part of the taxable base above €2,000/month, 10% below.
budget_policy_preset_food9_tip: Move food to the reduced VAT rate — the most often proposed VAT change.
budget_policy_preset_restaurants9_tip: Restore the reduced rate for restaurants and catering that expired at end-2024.
budget_policy_preset_nocap_tip: Remove the МОД ceiling so contributions apply to the full wage.
budget_policy_preset_cpionly_tip: Index pensions by inflation only instead of the 50/50 Swiss rule.
budget_policy_preset_admin10_tip: Cut a tenth of administration positions — vacancies absorb most of it.
budget_policy_preset_ssp_tip: Under КСО art. 6(5) the budget currently pays both contribution shares for ≈133k civil servants; this shifts the standard employee share (13.78%) onto them, uncompensated.
budget_policy_preset_maternity1_tip: Drop the paid second year of maternity leave (€398.81/mo) — floated by a ПБ MP on 1 June 2026; the party officially disavowed it the same day.
budget_policy_proj_macro: Assumptions for {{year}}: GDP {{gdp}}, real growth {{growth}}%, inflation {{hicp}}%, unemployment {{unemp}}% (EC Spring 2026 forecast).
budget_policy_proj_note: General-government sector (ESA 2010), not the cash КФП grain; the 2025 bar is the НСИ outturn. Baseline: the EC Spring 2026 forecast (balance −4.1% in 2026 and −4.3% in 2027; on 3 June 2026 the EC recommended an excessive-deficit procedure), then no-policy-change with a constant primary balance. Debt follows the standard debt-dynamics recursion with no stock-flow adjustments; interest: ~3.0% on the inherited stock (rolling over at ~12%/yr) and 3.6% on new debt; "Scenario interest" is the accumulated difference in interest costs vs the no-change path. The scenario's annual effect grows with nominal GDP, and pension indexation is recomputed for each horizon year from baseline-year inputs; effects apply from the start of the year — a policy enacted mid-2026 would have a proportionally smaller first-year effect. Static mode carries no behavioral response; in dynamic mode the projection also includes the GDP feedback (multipliers decaying over the horizon).
budget_policy_comparator: The total change equals {{health}}% of annual general-government health spending, or {{education}}% of education spending ({{year}}).
budget_policy_sentence: Scenario: {{changes}} — effect {{total}}/yr on the budget balance ({{pct}}% of GDP).
budget_policy_incidence_caption: Net monthly effect (pay + VAT on spending) by gross salary, employee profile.
budget_policy_tip_exp: Spending levers from the budget debate. Their effects flow into the same headline as the tax levers — as the impact on the budget balance.
budget_policy_tip_swiss: The Swiss rule indexes pensions each July by 50% inflation + 50% insurable-income growth. Shift the weight: 100% = CPI-only (cheaper while wages outgrow prices), 0% = income-only.
budget_policy_nosupp: Do not index the COVID supplement
budget_policy_tip_nosupp: The 60 лв supplement folded into pension bases in July 2022. Excluding that slice from indexation trims each round's cost.
budget_policy_tip_admin: Share of administration positions eliminated. Vacant positions absorb cuts first and save almost nothing; real layoffs also return part of the saving as lost income tax and contributions.
budget_policy_tip_mrz: Кодекс на труда чл. 244 pegs next year's МРЗ to 50% of the average gross wage. Freezing it cuts the budget two ways: it forgoes the contributions + income tax on the private wages the formula would have lifted (a loss), but avoids the payroll the budget itself pays its own low-wage staff (≈{{share}}% of the affected wage mass — a saving). The row shows the net; freezing turns net-positive only above ~31% public share.
budget_policy_mrz_note: Private SSC + tax forgone {{loss}}; public payroll avoided {{save}}
budget_policy_admin_note: ({{vac}}% of the cut falls on vacant positions)
budget_policy_tip_def: Target defense spending as % of GDP under the NATO definition (currently ~2.2%; differs from the national-accounts COFOG figure by military pensions and equipment timing). Priced against the projected GDP.
budget_policy_tip_wi: Indexes the consolidated personnel bill (wages + contributions, €6.3B executed). The 2026 draft proposed +5% with exempted sectors.
budget_policy_wex: Exempt военни, полицаи, лекари, учители (≈55% of the bill)
budget_policy_tip_kap: Changes PLANNED capital expenditure; the cash effect is scaled by the historical execution rate ({{rate}}%) — plans that would not have been executed cost or save nothing when changed on paper.
budget_policy_ssp: Civil servants pay their own contributions
budget_policy_tip_ssp: Under КСО art. 6(5) the budget pays both contribution shares for ≈133 thousand people — civil servants, the judiciary, the military, МВР and the special services. The lever shifts the standard employee share (13.78%) onto them, as every other employee pays; the elevated special-category rates stay budget-paid. Without compensation the saving is the employee share net of lost income tax; realistic reforms gross salaries up.
budget_policy_tip_hp: Percentage points added to the 8% health-insurance contribution, collected on the employee insurable base (€31.5B).
budget_policy_tip_mp: Pensions below the minimum are topped up to it. Raising the minimum pension costs the top-up to the new floor for every pensioner below it — over НОИ's distribution of pensioners by basic monthly pension size.
budget_policy_tip_tp: The "125% of the average wage" policy pegs teachers' pay to the economy-wide average. The current ratio uses the public-education sector average wage as a proxy — it includes non-teaching staff too. The cost includes employer contributions.
budget_policy_frag_ssp_gross: civil servants pay own contributions (grossed up)
budget_policy_frag_tp: teachers' pay at {{v}}% of the average wage
budget_policy_tip_mat: The child-raising benefit for ages 1–2 is €398.81/mo (frozen by the extension law); the program cost €154M in 2025 (НОИ). Cutting it from 2 years to 1 was floated by a ПБ MP on 1 June 2026; the party officially disavowed it the same day — here you can price it. Dynamic mode adds the income tax + contributions of the mothers who return to work earlier (central 45%, a recapture on top of the saving; the 50%-if-working rule informs the band); static mode ignores it.
budget_policy_tip_mpf: The base salary is 3× the public-sector average wage (€4,236 since May 2026), recalculated quarterly; on 11 June 2026 the legal committee approved the freeze unanimously. The saving is one year of foregone growth on the pay mass of 240 MPs with extras; the president, PM and ministers ride the same base and would add a small extra saving, not modeled.
budget_policy_tip_psub: Since 30 April 2026 the subsidy is €3.00 per valid vote (cut from €4.09 = 8 лв, adopted 3 June 2026); ~2.86M subsidized votes across 7 formations ≈ €8.6M per year.
budget_pwc_rates_source: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries — EU member-state rates behind the simulator's "Like in…" comparators
budget_nato_defence_source: NATO — annual defence-expenditure compendium (allies' % of GDP)
budget_ec_forecast_source: European Commission — economic forecast for Bulgaria (the base of the 5-year balance/debt projection)
budget_nsi_edp_source: НСИ — EDP notification on deficit and debt (the simulator projection's starting point)
budget_ec_vat_gap_source: European Commission — VAT gap report (the anchor of the dynamic mode's VAT response)
budget_imf_weo_source: IMF — World Economic Outlook for Bulgaria (the anchor of the dynamic mode's fiscal multipliers)
budget_fiscal_council_source: Fiscal Council — opinions and publications (the simulator's benchmark costings and the dividend-lever calibration)
budget_policy_tip_corp: A single 10% rate on profits (since 2007). Static scaling of corporate receipts; ignores profit shifting and behavioral response.
budget_policy_discuss: Discuss your scenario in the Наясно group
budget_policy_affect_nm: Touches {{pct}} of employees — monthly taxable base below the threshold.
budget_policy_affect_b2: The second rate touches {{pct}} of employees.
budget_policy_affect_mod: Touches {{pct}} of employees — wages above the cap.
budget_policy_affect_mat: The second year is used by ≈ {{n}} mothers a year.
budget_policy_affect_mp: ≈ {{n}} pensioners get topped up to the new minimum.
budget_policy_affect_ssp: Touches ≈ {{n}} people — administration, judiciary, defense and security.
budget_policy_public_submit: Add your scenario to the public tally
budget_policy_public_submit_tip: Sends your lever settings (no personal data) to the simulator's public statistics. Once per scenario.
budget_policy_public_edp: {{pct}}% of scenarios bring the deficit under 3% of GDP
budget_policy_public_few: Not enough scenarios for statistics yet — add yours.
budget_policy_public_dev_blocked: Submitting is disabled in dev mode (the proxy points at production).
budget_policy_public_caption: Voluntarily submitted scenarios; the figures (effect, median, shares) are computed in each visitor's own browser and are not centrally verified. Not a representative sample. No personal data is stored.
budget_policy_tip_mode_static: Static scoring: bases held fixed, no behavioral response — the convention of official costings.
budget_policy_tip_mode_dynamic: Dynamic scoring: adds behavioral base responses (reporting, shifting, compliance), a macro feedback on GDP and a 90% uncertainty band.
budget_policy_goal_edp_tip: Bring the deficit under 3% of GDP — the threshold behind the EC's excessive-deficit-procedure recommendation (2026-06-03).
budget_policy_goal_edp_desc: Deficit under 3% of GDP in {{year}} — close the excessive-deficit gap
budget_policy_goal_debt_tip: Keep government debt under 40% of GDP at the end of the projection. The baseline drifts to ~43%.
budget_policy_goal_debt_desc: Government debt under 40% of GDP in {{year}}
budget_policy_goal_def_tip: Reach 3% of GDP on defense (NATO definition) without a first-year deficit worse than the baseline.
budget_policy_goal_def_desc: Defense at 3% of GDP without worsening the {{year}} balance vs the baseline
budget_policy_goal_def_note: The defense lever sits at {{v}}% of GDP — the mission needs ≥ 3.0% and a balance no worse than the baseline.
budget_policy_decile_title: Who gains, who loses — by employee decile
budget_policy_decile_caption: Mean monthly change (net pay + VAT on spending) for each tenth of wage earners ordered by gross salary — employees only, over a wage distribution validated against the 2023 НАП data.
budget_policy_tip_total: Static estimate against the {{year}} executed budget: bases held fixed, only rates and rules change. Positive = the balance improves.
budget_policy_hero_range: {{low}} … {{high}} — 9 in 10 simulations fall within this band
budget_policy_tip_total_dyn: Dynamic estimate against the {{year}} executed budget: the static effect plus behavioral base responses and a year-1 GDP feedback. Positive = the balance improves.
budget_policy_tip_deficit: The consolidated cash deficit for {{year}}, after your levers. The baseline is the EC spring forecast (−4.1% of GDP); the government's own draft budget projects a higher deficit — about −5.7% of GDP (≈ −€7.2B). The sliders show how the measures move the balance against the baseline.
budget_policy_tip_excise_tobacco_row: The dynamic estimate accounts for illicit and cross-border substitution — on a steep hike the revenue can land well below the static figure.
budget_policy_tip_gambling_row: The dynamic estimate accounts for migration to unlicensed/offshore operators — at high rates the revenue can land well below the static figure.
budget_policy_tip_road_charges_row: The dynamic estimate nets out heavy-vehicle cross-border diversion and the collection ramp on the uplift — small, since car vignettes barely respond.
budget_policy_tip_soe_row: The face-value budget effect of cutting BDZh/NRIC/Post subsidies. Real feasibility is lower — much is locked in public-service and infrastructure contracts.
budget_policy_tip_mod_row: Includes the income-tax interaction: a different cap changes the contributions deducted from the taxable base.
budget_policy_no_change: Current law — move a slider to score a scenario.
budget_policy_baseline_note: Baseline: {{year}} executed revenue — VAT {{vat}}, personal income tax {{pit}}. Sources: КФП monthly execution, НАП annual report, Eurostat national accounts.
budget_policy_citizen_caption: Employee profile, no child relief, 75% of net spent on VAT-carrying consumption. The full per-person breakdown lives in the tax calculator.
budget_top_deviations_subtitle: Spending units whose execution diverged most from the original budget law, fiscal year
budget_top_deviations_caption: Plan = original ЗДБРБ appropriation. Executed = year-end execution report (Отчет за изпълнението на програмния бюджет). Only ministries with both figures and a planned budget over €20M are shown.
budget_journey_subtitle: Law → execution → audit, per fiscal year.
budget_methodology_description: Where the budget data comes from, how it is processed, and what the dashboard covers.
budget_meth_source_p: The figures come from the Ministry of Finance dataset “state budget execution by major budget indicators”, published on the national open-data portal data.egov.bg under a public-domain (CC0) licence. The Ministry publishes one resource per monthly cash-execution snapshot; each lists, for the five top-level sections of the state budget, the amount set by the budget law and the amount executed so far that fiscal year.
budget_meth_sections_p: The Bulgarian state budget execution table has five top-level sections, and the dashboard tracks each of them over time:
budget_meth_revenue: — tax and non-tax revenue, grants and donations.
budget_meth_expenditure: — spending and transfers: personnel, operations, capital, social spending, subsidies, interest.
budget_meth_eu: — Bulgaria's contribution to the common EU budget.
budget_meth_balance: — revenue minus expenditure minus the EU contribution. Negative means a deficit.
budget_meth_financing: — how the balance is financed: external and domestic borrowing, deposits, privatisation.
budget_meth_currency_p: Bulgaria adopted the euro on 1 January 2026. Monthly snapshots up to and including December 2025 are published in leva; from January 2026 they are in euro. Every figure on the dashboard is shown in euro.
budget_meth_cumulative_p: Each monthly snapshot is cumulative year-to-date: the December figure is the whole year, and the series resets each January. The execution-trend chart shows this directly — a ramp within each fiscal year, then a reset.
budget_meth_integrity_p: The ingest fails loudly rather than publish partial data: a pinned snapshot is re-parsed on every run and byte-compared against a committed fixture, so any drift in the parser is caught before anything is written, and a change touching more than a small fraction of the data aborts the run for review.
budget_meth_ministries_p: Each first-level spending unit (ministry, agency, independent body) publishes its own annual execution report. The ingest parses these PDFs into a uniform admin-grain table and reconciles them against the State Budget Law, so each ministry page shows planned versus actual at the ministry level and one column deeper at the program level.
budget_meth_documents_p: Each fiscal year is linked to its source documents — the State Budget Law as promulgated in Държавен вестник, the mid-year amendment laws, the year-end execution report, and the report of the National Audit Office. The fiscal-year page surfaces the timeline and the original links.
budget_meth_procurement_p: Each spending unit's page links to the public-procurement contracts it awarded under the Public Procurement Act, so a budget line can be followed all the way down to the individual contracts that consumed it.

chart_event_gfc_desc: Contraction of world trade and capital flows after the Lehman Brothers collapse (Sep 2008). For Bulgaria — sharp GDP fall and unemployment spike through 2009–2010.
chart_event_borisov1_protests_desc: Anti-monopoly and electricity-bill protests in Feb–Mar 2013 that brought down GERB's first cabinet four months before the regular election.
chart_event_dans_desc: Year-long civic mobilisation against the Oresharski cabinet, started days after Delyan Peevski was appointed head of DANS (June 2013).
chart_event_ktb_desc: Bank run and licence withdrawal of Corporate Commercial Bank in summer 2014 — Bulgaria's largest banking crisis since the 1990s. Hit the fiscal reserve and trust in the banking supervisor.
chart_event_covid_desc: Global pandemic from the WHO declaration (March 2020) to the lifting of Bulgaria's epidemic state of emergency (April 2022). Drove the 2020 GDP contraction and the 2021–2023 inflation cycle.
chart_event_borisov3_protests_desc: Anti-government and anti-corruption protests through summer and autumn 2020 — did not topple the cabinet but reshaped the 2021 election cycle.
chart_event_autumn_2025_protests_desc: Civic protests in autumn 2025, during the first year of the Zhelyazkov cabinet's term.

machine_only_title: Machine-only voting: who wins and who loses
machine_only_description: A test on Bulgaria's own election data of a reform ПрБ has already put before parliament: remove paper ballots in large sections, and watch national vote share and parliamentary seats recompute live.
machine_only_intro1: Since 2021 every Bulgarian parliamentary section has recorded machine votes and paper votes side by side — the same electorate, the same day, split only by the medium each voter chose. That split is a natural experiment: within a single section, machine-voters and paper-voters picked parties in measurably different proportions.
machine_only_intro_real: This is not just a thought experiment. On 7 July 2026 the governing party, Progressive Bulgaria (ПрБ), formally submitted exactly this proposal to parliament — fully machine voting, with paper ballots kept only in sections under 300 voters (plus mobile, hospital and ship sections). <src>See the reported bill</src>. The tool below lets you test what that reform would have done to the most recent result.
machine_only_intro2: The concrete question: what if paper ballots were removed in every "large" section — above some number of registered voters — forcing those voters onto the machine? Set a section-size threshold and how many paper-voters would simply stay home rather than use a machine, and everything below recomputes instantly against the most recent election. (Progressive Bulgaria's own bill draws the line at 300.)
machine_only_finding: machine voting pulls votes away from ГЕРБ-СДС and ДПС and channels them mainly to ПП-ДБ (and, to a lesser degree, ПрБ). The likely reason is who each group is — the machine is preferred by younger, urban voters, paper by older voters and people in smaller places. Explore where, and by how much, below.
machine_only_ctrl_caption: At this setting, {{sections}} sections ({{registered}} registered voters) go machine-only. Smaller sections keep paper as recorded.
machine_only_ctrl_adoption: Machine was already the freely-chosen medium of {{adoption}}% of voters in those sections — the lower that share, the more the model extrapolates from a self-selected minority.
machine_only_h_voteshare: The {{year}} parliamentary election — results vs. model
machine_only_seats_lede: Both columns are allocated with the same national method (Hare-Niemeyer) and 4% threshold, so the difference is purely the vote shift — not a change of allocation rules.
machine_only_seats_note: "Actual" seats are re-allocated with the same national Hare-Niemeyer method for a like-for-like comparison against the model; for this election that reproduces the official CEC seat totals exactly.
machine_only_map_lede: Each of the 31 electoral districts is coloured by the party that wins it under the model; the darker the shade, the more decisive the win. Hover a district for the full picture — whether the winner flips versus the actual result, and which parties move most.
machine_only_method_p1: In each affected section we treat the observed machine-voters as revealing that section's true preference. The paper-voters are then recast onto that same machine-vote distribution — the assumption being that, denied a paper ballot, they would use the machine and behave like their neighbours who already did.
machine_only_method_p2: The turnout dial d is the share of a section's paper-voters who abstain instead of switching. At d = 0 turnout is held constant (everyone switches); at d = 100% every paper-voter in a large section stays home and only the original machine-voters count.
machine_only_method_step1: Flag each section with more registered voters than the threshold that also has real machine votes.
machine_only_method_step2: Keep its machine-voters as cast, and redistribute its paper-voters by the section's own machine-vote shares, scaled by (1 − d).
machine_only_method_step3: Leave all smaller sections — and the few large sections whose machine failed — exactly as recorded.
machine_only_method_step4: Re-aggregate the resulting votes — nationally (for the result and the Hare-Niemeyer seat allocation) and by electoral district (for the map) — and do the same for the actual result so the two can be compared.
machine_only_caveat_behavior: It assumes paper-voters would vote the same way as their section's machine-voters. This captures the selection skew between the two groups, but not any change of choice caused by the medium itself.
machine_only_caveat_adoption: How telling the experiment is depends on how far machine voting has taken hold. In Bulgaria machine is already the freely-chosen medium of about half the affected voters — but the further back you look the weaker the signal (in April 2021 it was only ~29%, a young/urban early-adopter minority), so read the machine-share extrapolation as directional, not exact.
machine_only_caveat_turnout: The drop-off is a scenario dial, not an estimate — we have no clean empirical figure for how many paper-voters would abstain from voting under mandatory machine voting. A plausible band is roughly 10–30%; the extremes are there to bound the effect.
machine_only_caveat_seats: Seats are allocated by the national Hare-Niemeyer quota with the 4% threshold — the very method Bulgaria uses to fix each party's national total, which reproduces the official result for every election since 2013. (Bulgaria then spreads those totals across the 31 districts; that only changes where seats land, not how many each party wins.) The meaningful figure here is the seat difference between the actual result and the model.
machine_only_caveat_nomachine: Large sections abroad or with a broken machine (no machine votes recorded) are left as paper — the model cannot infer machine behaviour where none exists.
machine_only_caveat_none: Shares are computed on party votes only; the "I support no one" option is excluded.
machine_only_footer: Data: per-section protocols from the Central Election Commission, as processed by electionsbg.com. This is an analytical scenario, not a forecast.
